description_long: This is a medieval-style sword featuring a straight, double-edged blade with a pointed tip, suitable for thrusting and slashing. The blade is metallic, with a dark, slightly matte finish and visible ridges running along its length, suggesting reinforcement or decorative fullers. The crossguard is simple and functional, made of a brass or bronze-colored metal, providing hand protection. The handle is cylindrical, wrapped or textured for grip, and ends in a rounded pommel that matches the crossguard in color and material. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible engravings or ornamentation, and the proportions suggest it is a one-handed sword. The object appears robust and well-balanced, intended for practical use rather than display.
description: A straight, double-edged medieval sword with a dark metallic blade, brass-colored crossguard, and a rounded pommel. The handle is cylindrical and textured for grip.
description_view_0: Angled view showing the full length of the sword, highlighting the straight blade, crossguard, and rounded pommel.
description_view_1: Top-down view emphasizing the narrow profile of the blade and the rounded end of the pommel.
description_view_2: Opposite angled view similar to view 0, showing the blade, crossguard, and handle from the other side.
description_view_3: Side view displaying the thinness of the blade and the alignment of the handle, crossguard, and pommel.
attribute_handle_design: cylindrical, dark, textured or wrapped, rounded pommel
